description: Intent-to-orchestrator routing table. When Claude classifies user intent, this policy decides which primary orchestrator handles it. Supports keyword matching + tie-break rules. v1.1.0 adds discoverability_release_gate (L12) intent below appsec_narrow_* and above pentest_active. Not enforced at runtime; consulted as guidance.

intent_to_orchestrator.notes: Suggest `/claude-env-bootstrap` to the user; do NOT auto-execute. Has side effects (writes files, copies skills, modifies CLAUDE.md). disable-model-invocation: true.

intent_to_orchestrator.position: upstream of gsd-pipeline-orchestrator (pm_delivery) — I2R is the requirements front-end that feeds GSD
intent_to_orchestrator.notes: Requirements front-end UPSTREAM of GSD. Turns a raw/messy idea into a Markdown-first requirements package (WHAT/WHY + CONSTRAINTS + LOCKED DECISIONS only; NEVER HOW / tasks / architecture / UI / roadmap). SKILL-direct (not workflow-spec), all-opus, auto-invocable. Downstream (GSD etc.) reads the same Markdown package and applies its own routing; I2R emits no /gsd command. When intent is implementation/coding/UI/roadmap (the HOW/WHEN/WHO), route to gsd-pipeline-orchestrator instead — see do_not_trigger_on. Tie-break: a raw-idea / requirements-clarification request precedes pm_delivery (clarify WHAT before GSD derives HOW).

intent_keywords: 改 bug 但牵连面广
intent_to_orchestrator.notes: Default entry for all non-trivial work. Dispatches phase commands (spec / discuss / plan / execute / verify / ship). Upstream requirements clarification (raw idea → PRD) belongs to idea-to-requirements-orchestrator (see idea_to_requirements above).

create/optimize tasks run the v2.3 combination dispatch engine (uiux-product-orchestrator references/combination-policy.md): ask-first tier (min/optimal/max, default optimal) then a 6-phase state machine GROUND(reference-grounding hard pre-step; local design-system 58-brand corpus first, web fallback)->EXPLORE(multi-style sampling for user to pick, NOT a single skill)->PICK(user locks exactly one L3)->BUILD->UNIFY->REVIEW(loop, no early exit). This is a skill COMBINATION across layers, NOT single-skill dispatch. E4 client-proposal stays on sens-frontend-design (protected, unchanged). E6 local-patch / E7 audit use lightweight paths. Resolves L3 ambiguity by asking the user; L3 mutex enforced at PICK only (EXPLORE sampling is exempt).
9-layer test matrix decision tree. Includes AppSec routing for backend/API/auth changes.
QA load / stress / soak / spike reliability layer (k6 etc.). Dispatched under enterprise-qa-testing; not a standalone release gate.
QA mutation-testing / test-effectiveness layer (Stryker / cargo-mutants / mutmut / PIT). Dispatched under enterprise-qa-testing.

QA mobile / native-app E2E layer (Maestro etc.). DORMANT / 备选 — only activates when the project carries a mobile marker (android/ ios/ React Native / Flutter / Expo). Does NOT trigger for web-only projects.

RED-LINE planning-first double-gate chaos engineering / fault injection (Toxiproxy / Pumba; steady-state hypothesis + blast-radius). Staging only, NEVER production. Dispatched under enterprise-qa-testing; not a standalone release gate.

intent_to_orchestrator.routing_semantics: primary-orchestrator-first-then-delegate — appsec-security-orchestrator owns the entry; it consults sub_orchestrators based on narrower intent keywords (see appsec_narrow_routes below) and delegates rather than executing in-place. If the resolver supports narrow-intent direct routing, prefer the appsec_narrow_* intents below.
intent_to_orchestrator.notes: Defensive review and baseline only. Routes through 6-layer capability map (governance / app / platform / operations / response / compliance). Standardized finding schema in orchestrator §9. Active testing requires manual gate (pentest-scope-and-roe + authorized-pentest-validation).
Direct narrow route to threat-modeling skill. If parent appsec_defensive matches first, delegation to this skill must happen via orchestrator §5.1 governance.threat_modeling row.
Direct narrow route to secrets engineering skill.

Direct narrow route to IaC/cloud posture skill. Runtime cloud/cluster commands require user explicit confirmation (Tier B per skill §4). §4.5 k8s runtime authoring (Kyverno / OPA Gatekeeper admission control / Tetragon / Security Profiles Operator / seccomp / AppArmor) is conditional-k8s (only when a k8s marker is present) and strictly defensive.

Direct narrow route to supply-chain skill (SBOM gen + provenance/signing + deep SCA; deepened with reachability-based noise reduction + Prowler CSPM cloud posture). The §16 Step 4 npm/pip/cargo/trivy audit remains the inline floor.
Mobile security overlay. Coordinates with security-platform-secrets (Keychain/Keystore).
LLM + Agentic AI overlay. LLM Top 10 ALONE is insufficient — covers tool perms / memory poisoning / human override / rollback.
Multi-tenant overlay. 12-layer tenant boundary checklist.
WebSocket/SSE overlay. HTTP access log INSUFFICIENT for WS — frame-level controls required.
File upload overlay. 12-step OWASP cheat sheet defense.
Direct narrow route to API security skill (OWASP API Top 10:2023, per-endpoint authz for REST / GraphQL / gRPC).
Direct narrow route to coverage-guided / structure-aware fuzzing skill (libFuzzer / AFL++ / cargo-fuzz / Atheris / Jazzer / ClusterFuzzLite). Authors fuzz harness + corpus; defensive only.
Direct narrow route to deep interprocedural taint/dataflow SAST skill (CodeQL / Joern / semgrep taint). Source→sink reachability; deeper than the inline §16 Step floor.

RED-LINE double-gate authenticated/logged-in DAST (ZAP AF browser auth-context + spider + scan, Nuclei-with-session). Middle layer between dast-baseline-scanning (passive) and authorized-pentest-validation (exploitation). HARD-REQUIRES completed .planning/PENTEST-ROE.md + in-scope authorized staging/preview/lab target + active time window + explicit human authorization before any authenticated active step. Wrapper-only (raw ZAP/Nuclei CLI forbidden). NEVER auto-scans; no production; references (never bypasses/weakens) the frozen-name gates dast-baseline-scanning + authorized-pentest-validation.
PCI DSS 4.0.1 overlay. Prefers SAQ A architecture; never stores CVV/track/PIN.
China data protection overlay. Covers PIPL + DSL + CSL + CAC export rules + 2024 new regulations + GenAI registration.

Privacy compliance overlay for GDPR/CCPA/CPRA (ex-China). Composes alongside cn-data + payment, split by jurisdiction. De-conflict: China terms (PIPL / 数据出境 / 网信办 / CAC) stay with appsec_narrow_cn_data.
NIST SP 800-61 R3 IR workflow. Includes forensics bridge per NIST SP 800-86 until standalone forensics skill exists.
NIST CSF 2.0 RC function. 3 modes: post-incident / scheduled drill / recovery audit. Activates after incident-response RS→RC transition criteria met.

PLANNING + COVERAGE MAPPING ONLY — strictly defensive, never executes attacks/emulation/stealth/persistence. Active validation stays behind the manual authorized-pentest-validation gate, which this skill never invokes. NIST CSF 2.0 DE+RS; MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise (v19 = 15 tactics) + ATLAS.

Render-only security diagrams from existing .appsec/ + manifest fact-sources. NOT code architecture (that is arch-viz). Adds no data collection, never scans. 4 live diagrams; 8 planned (blocked on fact-sources).

intent_keywords: AI search 和 SEO 一起做
我的网站怎么被 ChatGPT 找到
我的网站怎么被 Google 找到

narrow_skill_keyword_extensions._comment: Wave B (2026-06-15) post-launch measurement / CI extensions on existing narrow skills. web-aeo §20 AI citation tracking + app-aso §16.6 ASO measurement are measurement-only (never gate inputs); web-seo §15 tech-SEO CI feeds the seo evidence channel.

intent_to_orchestrator.notes: L12 release gate; downstream of UIUX orchestrator. Narrow domain words (SEO / AEO / GEO / ASO alone) route directly to narrow skills, not here. `discoverability-growth` is growth-EXECUTION (keyword strategy / content-gap / programmatic SEO) — NOT a 5th audit channel and adds NO new evidence channel or gate. `disc-measurement-puller` pulls real post-launch metrics (GSC / GA4 / Bing / ASC) via discoverability-sdk measure.pull/measure.compare — measurement-only, NEVER a gate input (gate.check ignores measurement.json). Wave B narrow_skill_keyword_extensions add post-launch measurement (web-aeo §20 citation tracking, app-aso §16.6 ASO measurement — both measurement-only) + CI (web-seo §15 tech-SEO CI, feeds seo channel).

Templated recon + authorized vuln scan (nuclei / amass / ffuf / httpx). ROE-gated: planning + authorized scan only, behind the pentest-scope-and-roe + authorized-pentest-validation double-gate. Never runs active scan without ROE + user sign-off.

AI / agentic red team (prompt injection / tool abuse / memory poisoning; PyRIT / promptfoo redteam; OWASP Agentic Top 10). Planning-only — never executes attacks; any active validation stays behind the pentest-scope-and-roe + authorized-pentest-validation double-gate.

Planning/reference-only exploitation + adversary-simulation planning (Metasploit / Sliver / Caldera / Pacu / BloodHound). Read-only. Routes AFTER security-pentest-recon-scan. References the manual authorized-pentest-validation hard gate but is NEVER a bridge to it and NEVER auto-fires active/post-exploitation work — all active validation stays behind the pentest-scope-and-roe + authorized-pentest-validation double-gate.
MUST complete ROE first via pentest-scope-and-roe (visible governance skill). Active validation via authorized-pentest-validation is manual-only (disable-model-invocation: true).

tie_break_rules.description: When multiple orchestrators match, prefer the one with the most specific keyword match (e.g. 'pentest' is more specific than 'security' or 'testing').
When intent includes 'pentest' / 'active scan' / 'exploit', ALWAYS gate through pentest-scope-and-roe first regardless of other matches.
When intent involves backend/API/auth/data + testing, route to enterprise-qa-testing which will further route to appsec-security-orchestrator. Do NOT route directly to appsec from QA intent.
When intent is unclear, route to gsd-pipeline-orchestrator (PM main line) which can dispatch via gsd-spec-phase to clarify WHAT is being built.
For 1-3 line bugfixes / answering questions / reading code / 'don't use GSD' explicit user requests, skip orchestrators entirely.

utility_skill_routes._comment: Supporting utility skills (code-reading 2026-05-25; arch-viz + env-parity-baseline added 2026-06-02). Lightweight tools — NOT orchestrators, NOT gates, NOT sources of truth. Keyword-only routing. They never replace primary orchestrators; they feed supporting context into GSD / UIUX / QA / AppSec / L12. Covers code-reading (gitnexus / codegraph), architecture visualization (arch-viz), and cross-env deployment parity (env-parity-baseline).

gitnexus-repo-map.purpose: Repo-level visual map / architecture topology / cluster overview / unfamiliar open-source reading. Wraps GitNexus CLI; CLI-first; web UI opt-in; never auto-runs gitnexus setup; never modifies global ~/.claude/.
do_not_use_for: Local symbol callers / callees / impact — route to codegraph-cli instead
Decision-making — route to GSD / UIUX / QA / AppSec / L12 orchestrators

codegraph-cli.purpose: Local symbol-level traversal — callers / callees / impact / affected tests / task context. Wraps CodeGraph CLI; CLI-only by default; MCP off by default; never runs codegraph install automatically.
keywords: 改这个函数会影响什么
do_not_use_for: Visual / architectural / repo-wide view — route to gitnexus-repo-map instead
QA release gate — affected output is supporting context only, gate stays in enterprise-qa-testing
Spec / product decisions — route to gsd-spec-phase

arch-viz.purpose: Repo → committable, presentable architecture bundle (interactive offline viewer viz/index.html + agent-readable graph.json + architecture.svg + ARCHITECTURE.md) written into docs/architecture/. Wraps the local arch-viz CLI. Use for shareable / committable architecture artifacts for clients / teammates; prefer over gitnexus-repo-map when a permissive committable artifact is wanted.
do_not_use_for: Local symbol callers / callees / impact — route to codegraph-cli instead
Quick throwaway reading of an unfamiliar repo — gitnexus-repo-map is lighter
Decision-making — route to GSD / UIUX / QA / AppSec / L12 orchestrators

env-parity-baseline.purpose: Cross-environment parity baseline & deployment risk router. Detects dev / CI / staging / prod consistency gaps, injects a minimum baseline set (.gitattributes / .editorconfig / env-validator / CI matrix / env-contract / runtime-manifest / deploy-parity-checklist), and routes deployment-layer concerns (Docker / Compose / k8s / systemd / Nginx / Terraform / Ansible) to downstream specialists. Scope = detect + minimum contract + handoff, NOT a full deploy generator. Twelve-Factor X (dev/prod parity) + III (config in env) aligned.
do_not_use_for: Full Docker / k8s / Terraform generation — env-parity only detects + hands off to deployment specialists
Security secret scanning — route to security-platform-secrets / appsec-security-orchestrator
CI test strategy — route to enterprise-qa-testing

codex-dispatch.purpose: Cross-AI dispatch playbook — the harness decision + discipline layer for using Codex (OpenAI codex CLI, GPT 系列) as a second model family. Does NOT shell out to `codex exec`; routes all execution to the official codex@openai-codex plugin commands (/codex:rescue delegate · /codex:review · /codex:adversarial-review · /codex:status · /codex:result · /codex:cancel · /codex:setup). Owns the harness-specific discipline the generic plugin lacks: when-to-delegate decision tree, work-order template, chunking/scope caps, mandatory wave cross-review, Windows UTF-8 caveat, quota fallback, model-version lock, governed-gate boundary (Codex review = advisory, NEVER release verdict).
do_not_use_for: Running codex itself — execution is the official codex@openai-codex plugin's job (this skill only decides + routes)
Release / security / QA gate verdict — Codex review is advisory-only scout; verdict stays in the deterministic spec-runner + spec_hash approval (CLAUDE.md §3.7)
Codex prompt drafting internals — that is the plugin's built-in gpt-5-4-prompting skill
Enabling the review-gate stop hook — forbidden (burns quota; conflicts with governed-gate human sign-off)

pairing.rule: Both tools = repo reading / code context. They do NOT compete. On hard problems, GitNexus first (see the repo), then CodeGraph CLI (aim at a specific symbol).

manual_first_skills.reason: Has side effects (writes files, copies skills, modifies CLAUDE.md). User must invoke via /claude-env-bootstrap.
Performs active security validation. Must NEVER auto-trigger. Hard gate via disable-model-invocation: true.
Multi-surface production wave. Expensive (parallel subagents). User must explicitly request.
Experimental L3 style. User must opt in by name.

three_mainline_handoffs.trigger: UI changes affect theme/layout/image — notify for visual regression target
QA decision tree hits backend/API/auth/data branch
ROE 11-item checklist complete + user explicit sign-off
